finding: The field labelled DateTime_UTC mixes correct 12:30/13:30 UTC release rows with lagged 19:30/20:30 rows that are one calendar day early and isolated same-day 11:30, 12:32, and 15:00 corruptions.

fixed_rule.release_date: Use raw date + 1 day when parsed naive hour >= 18; otherwise use raw date.
fixed_rule.release_time: Construct 08:30 America/New_York on that release date.
fixed_rule.utc_conversion: Convert the timezone-aware release timestamp to UTC using America/New_York DST rules.
fixed_rule.postcondition: All selected macro releases normalize to 12:30 or 13:30 UTC.
fixed_rule.duplicate_resolution: For the same event name and normalized timestamp, prefer the row whose raw timestamp already equals normalized UTC; equal-quality ambiguity is a hard error.

pre_screen_amendment: The first validation pass covered the lagged and already-correct cases. A full selected-event audit then exposed isolated 11:30, 12:32, and 15:00 corruptions plus one conflicting Core PCE duplicate on 2019-04-29. The timestamp and duplicate rules were strengthened before any strategy screen was executed.
selection_use: The normalization rule was fixed before the macro surprise screen and is not a strategy parameter.
